package main import ( "errors" "fmt" "html" "mime" "strings" ) // BuildFeedbackEmail constructs a HTML-formatted email body for user feedback func BuildFeedbackEmail(fromAddr string, toAddr string, userName string, subject string, feedbackContent string) (emailBody string, err error) { // Validate required input parameters if fromAddr == "" { return "", errors.New("from address cannot be empty") } if toAddr == "" { return "", errors.New("to address cannot be empty") } if userName == "" { return "", errors.New("user name cannot be empty") } if feedbackContent == "" { return "", errors.New("feedback content cannot be empty") } // Encode email subject to handle non-ASCII characters correctly encodedSubject := mime.QEncoding.Encode("utf-8", subject) // Escape all user-provided content to prevent HTML injection escapedUserName := html.EscapeString(userName) escapedSubject := html.EscapeString(subject) escapedFeedbackContent := html.EscapeString(feedbackContent) // Use strings.Builder for efficient string concatenation var emailBuilder strings.Builder // Write email headers with proper CRLF line endings (per RFC 5322) emailBuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("From: %s\r\n", fromAddr)) emailBuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("To: %s\r\n", toAddr)) emailBuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("Subject: %s\r\n", encodedSubject)) emailBuilder.WriteString("MIME-Version: 1.0\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("Content-Type: text/html; charset=\"utf-8\"\r\n") // Blank line to separate headers from message body emailBuilder.WriteString("\r\n") // Write styled HTML email body emailBuilder.WriteString("\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("New User Feedback\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("
\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("

New User Feedback Submitted

\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("
\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("
\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("Username:") emailBuilder.WriteString("") emailBuilder.WriteString(escapedUserName) emailBuilder.WriteString("\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("
\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("
\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("Feedback Subject:") emailBuilder.WriteString("") emailBuilder.WriteString(escapedSubject) emailBuilder.WriteString("\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("
\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("
\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("Feedback Content:\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("
") emailBuilder.WriteString(escapedFeedbackContent) emailBuilder.WriteString("
\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("
\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("\r\n") emailBuilder.WriteString("\r\n") return emailBuilder.String(), nil } func main() { // Example usage of BuildFeedbackEmail fromAddress := "user@example.com" toAddress := "support@example.org" userName := "Alice Smith" feedbackSubject := "Suggestion for improving the dashboard UI 🎨" feedbackContent := `Hi Team, I've been using the dashboard for a few weeks now and I have some suggestions: 1. The loading time for the analytics section could be faster. 2. It would be great to have a dark mode option. 3. The export function currently only supports CSV, adding Excel would be helpful. Overall, I'm really happy with the product! Keep up the good work. Best regards, Alice` // Generate the email email, err := BuildFeedbackEmail(fromAddress, toAddress, userName, feedbackSubject, feedbackContent) if err != nil { fmt.Printf("Failed to build feedback email: %v\n", err) return } // Print the generated email (in a real scenario, you would send this via smtp.SendMail) fmt.Println("=== Generated Feedback Email ===") fmt.Println() fmt.Println(email) }
